items.title: Scientist 'shocked' $300m irrigation scheme pushing ahead without studies

children.content: A $300 million irrigation scheme will supply 37.2 gigalitres of water to farming areas near Hobart from 2030. A scientists fears the taxpayer-funded scheme could put a major river at risk.

gallery.altText: She stands by the river with a red silky scarf around her neck

thumbnail.altText: She stands by the river with a red silky scarf around her neck

items.title: 'There's one kid in every classroom': Teaching children to navigate grief

children.content: One in 20 Australians under the age of 18 has lost a parent, but families say grief services for children are limited. A national program teaching children how to navigate grief is trying to change that.

image.altText: A young boy being carried on the back of his mother.

items.title: ABC wins 11 categories at Tasmanian Media Awards

children.content: The ABC has won 11 of the 20 categories at this year's Media Entertainment & Arts Alliance (MEAA) Tasmanian Media Awards, while WIN News reporter Brent Costelloe took home the prestigious journalist of the year award.

image.altText: A group of people in suits and dresses holding awards and smiling for a photo.

items.title: 'Completely unrealistic' cuts trouble health groups, as treasurer searches for $700 million in savings

children.content: Health groups fear the impact of hundreds of millions of dollars in cuts expected to be found in the Health Department as part of Treasurer Eric Abetz's state budget operational efficiencies program.

gallery.altText: A female doctor carrying a stethoscope.

thumbnail.altText: A female doctor carrying a stethoscope.

items.title: When the bushfire came, this couple turned on their roof sprinkler and left

children.content: Preparing for an ember attack could extend your home's bushfire survivability rate to above 90 per cent or even closer to 100 per cent, a fire scientist says.

image.altText: Aerial perspective of a coastal home spinkler system on roof surrounded by burned trees under a blue sky.

items.title: 'Bushwalkers have died attempting this track': Instagram post glosses over hike's dangers

children.content: It looks beautiful in the Instagram post with blue skies and picturesque tarns carved by glaciers. But read further and the multi-day Tasmanian hike is considered one of Australia's most challenging bushwalks. In fact, it can be deadly.

image.altText: Procyon Peak, Western Arthur Range Dan Broun

items.title: Rex Airlines announces service cuts in Victoria and Tasmania

children.content: The embattled carrier has announced it is ceasing some services and reducing others.

image.altText: a plane flies in the sky

items.title: Giant iceberg with shipping containers spotted off Antarctica

children.content: Details of how the shipping containers ended up on a giant iceberg have been revealed in one of the more unusual reports tabled at the annual meeting of Antarctic Treaty nations.

image.altText: A large rectangular iceberg with seven dark dots on it, seen from a satellite photo.

items.title: Piastri starts fourth in cold and damp F1 Canada grand prix

children.content: Oscar Piastri starts on the second row of the grid for this morning's Formula 1 Canadian Grand Prix. Follow live.

image.altText: Oscar Piastri in his McLaren garage, looking upward

items.title: The once-promising AFL player hitting home runs in US Major League

children.content: As a teenager, Curtis Mead had to choose between an iconically Australian sport and America's pastime. He's now hitting home runs in Major League Baseball.

gallery.altText: Curtis Mead on the field during a baseball game.

has died following a shark attack in Far North Queensland.

items.title: At least 24 killed in Pakistan train blast claimed by separatist militants

children.content: A bomb blast has hit a shuttle train carrying Pakistani security personnel and their families in the latest major attack claimed by separatist militants.

image.altText: A group of men, including emergency workers, have gathered around the wreckage of a train on rail tracks

items.title: Giants create AFL history with onslaught, Dogs down Dees in classic

items.title: North Queensland crowd raises record figure for Arrow and MND charity

children.content: North Queensland climbs into the NRL's top six with an impressive 30-18 victory over South Sydney in Townsville, where fans raise thousands of dollars for the Fight MND charity.

image.altText: Jai Arrow looks on during a South Sydney game

items.title: Politics and faith collide over choice for Tibet's next Dalai Lama

children.content: Tibetan Buddhists believe the Dalai Lama decides when and where he will reincarnate. But China's ruling Communist Party says it must choose who will replace him.

items.title: Recent fall in auction clearance rates could be good news for first home buyers

items.title: My dog Teddy was more than just a pet. We needed each other

items.title: Trump skips son's wedding due to his 'love of the United States'

children.content: James Duckworth gets an easy path into the second round of the French Open after his opponent retires midway through the second set.

items.title: Man charged after 14yo restaurant worker threatened with knife

children.content: Police say a 30-year-old man who allegedly demanded money from a 14-year-old worker at knifepoint, on the NSW Central Coast, was tackled to the ground by two other men.

items.title: Former actor Rachel Ward became a farmhand at 65 and found her purpose

children.content: With the country wind in her grey hair, cow dung on her boots and not a skerrick of make-up, former actor Rachel Ward has found her place, sharing her passion for regenerative farming.

items.title: 'I've had enough': Why this loyal Labor voter is switching to One Nation

children.content: The next opportunity to test support for One Nation in Western Sydney will be the state election on March 13. Already, disillusioned Labor voter Silvana Rota is ready to "give them a go".

articles.title: Labor's tax defence is backfiring with young voters

children.content: Labor is banking on younger generations, who are overwhelmingly wage earners, being more interested in owning their own home than pocketing higher profits on a lucky crypto trade.

image.altText: Albanese is in focus as he sits on the green bench watching Chalmers speak

items.title: Russian missile strike kills four people in Kyiv

children.content: Russia uses a hypersonic Oreshnik ballistic missile to hit Kyiv, killing four people and injuring at least 80 others.

items.title: Maldives cave dive deaths leave us with many theories and one big question

children.content: The question of why experienced divers would descend to depths of more than 50 metres on a single tank of air — let alone enter a cave while doing so — baffles members of the relatively close-knit deep-sea diving community.

image.altText: A deep-sea diver descends into a cave system, trailing a guide line.

items.title: This private island paradise has a bargain price tag, and problems galore

children.content: Makri Island, not far from mainland Greece, boasts almost 100 hectares of uninhabited Mediterranean beauty, but buyers be warned, this slice of paradise comes with a catch.

image.altText: An island, seen from above

items.title: Fifty years ago, a killer returned the knife to the kitchen and slipped away

children.content: Teenage Dawn Hillier was a "ray of sunshine". Almost 50 years later, years of investigation, a coronial inquiry, and a big reward have been unable to solve her brutal murder.

items.title: Export name change coming for Australian prosecco

children.content: Australia is the only country outside Italy that can use the name prosecco, but its export labels will need to change in the coming years.

image.altText: A glass of white sparkling wine held up towards the sun
